title: Event Ingest Pipeline
description: A batch pipeline that ingests, validates, transforms, and loads events, with an explicit ticket dependency graph.
background: Downstream analytics need clean, deduplicated events. The pipeline stages must run in a strict order, which the ticket dependency graph encodes.

goals.title: Deliver clean events downstream
goals.description: Validate and deduplicate raw events before they reach analytics.
successCriteria: No invalid or duplicate event reaches the warehouse

goals.title: Run stages in a safe order
goals.description: Encode stage ordering so transform never runs before validation.
successCriteria: Each stage runs only after its prerequisites complete

goals.title: Make runs observable
goals.description: Emit per-stage metrics so operators can see where a run stands.
successCriteria: Each stage emits start, success, and failure metrics

requirements.title: Ingest raw events
requirements.description: Read raw events from the source bucket into the pipeline.

acceptanceCriteria.given: raw event files in the source bucket
acceptanceCriteria.then: all event files are read and staged for validation

requirements.title: Validate events
requirements.description: Reject events that do not match the event schema.

acceptanceCriteria.then: invalid events are quarantined and valid events proceed

requirements.title: Load events
requirements.description: Write transformed events into the warehouse.

acceptanceCriteria.then: events are written idempotently to the warehouse

architecture: A staged batch pipeline (ingest -> validate -> transform -> load) orchestrated as ordered jobs. Each stage is independently retryable and emits metrics.

techStack.name: Python
techStack.rationale: Rich data tooling for batch transformation.

folderStructures.content:
pipeline/
  ingest.py
  validate.py
  transform.py
  load.py
  metrics.py

nonFunctionalRequirements.description: The pipeline keeps up with daily event volume.

guardrails.description: The load stage must be idempotent.
guardrails.rationale: Stages can be retried after partial failures.
guardrails.consequence: A non-idempotent load would create duplicate warehouse rows on retry.

epics.title: Build the staged pipeline
epics.description: Implement each pipeline stage and wire the ordering between them.
epics.objective: A reliable ingest-to-load pipeline with explicit stage ordering.

tickets.title: Implement ingest stage
tickets.description: Read raw events from the source bucket and stage them.

tickets.title: Implement validate stage
tickets.description: Validate staged events and quarantine invalid ones.

tickets.title: Implement transform stage
tickets.description: Transform validated events into the warehouse shape.

implementationSteps.text: Map event fields to warehouse columns

tickets.title: Implement load stage
tickets.description: Idempotently load transformed events into the warehouse.
guardrails: Use MERGE to keep the load idempotent

tickets.title: End-to-end pipeline verification
tickets.description: Verify a full run produces clean warehouse rows from sample input.

acceptanceCriteria.then: the warehouse contains exactly the expected clean rows

implementationSteps.text: Run the pipeline against the sample fixture and assert warehouse contents

qualityGates: Warehouse output matches the golden fixture

blueprints.title: Pipeline stage ordering
blueprints.description: The strict ordering of pipeline stages.
blueprints.content:
flowchart LR
  ingest --> validate --> transform --> load
